message:
Command `# Step 38 still active (log at 12:57, 3+ min). 
# Antenna repair completed, now re-running global routing with fixed design.
# This GRT re-run for antenna fixes is typically fast (5-10 min).

WRAPPER_RUN="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
LOG="/tmp/librelane_final.log"
LR_PID=363476

for i in $(seq 1 120); do
  sleep 600

  LR_ALIVE=$(ps -p $LR_PID -o pid --no-headers 2>/dev/null | xargs)
  ELAPSED=$(ps -p $LR_PID -o etime --no-headers 2>/dev/null | xargs)
  STEP=$(ls -t "$WRAPPER_RUN/" 2>/dev/null | grep "^[0-9]" | head -1)
  STEP_COUNT=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/" 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^[0-9]" || echo 0)
  LOG_BYTES=$(stat -c%s "$LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  LOG_MTIME=$(stat -c%y "$LOG" 2>/dev/null | cut -c12-19)
  LOG_TAIL=$(tail -1 "$LOG" 2>/dev/null | xargs | cut -c1-80)
  FINAL_GDS=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/gds/"*.gds 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  FINAL_LEF=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/lef/"*.lef 2>/dev/null | head -1)

  echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] LR=${LR_ALIVE:-DONE}(${ELAPSED}) step=$STEP (#${STEP_COUNT}) log=$(numfmt --to=iec $LOG_BYTES 2>/dev/null) mtime=$LOG_MTIME"
  echo "  $LOG_TAIL"
  [ -n "$FINAL_GDS" ] && echo "  *** GDS: $FINAL_GDS ***"
  [ -n "$FINAL_LEF" ] && echo "  *** LEF: $FINAL_LEF ***"
  echo ""

  if [ -z "$LR_ALIVE" ]; then
    echo "=== LibreLane EXITED ==="
    ls -lt "$WRAPPER_RUN/" | head -60
    find "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/" 2>/dev/null | sort | head -40
    tail -80 "$LOG"
    break
  fi
done` executed with exit code -1.

message:
I am thinking...: 
A new error: `[DRT-0302] Unsupported multiple pins on bterm VDDIO`. This means the VDDIO power net has multiple pins on the boundary term (bterm), which TritonRoute doesn't support.

Looking at this error: the DEF template copies power pins from the padframe (`FP_TEMPLATE_COPY_POWER_PINS: true`), and VDDIO has multiple pad connections (it appears on multiple power pads in the padframe). TritonRoute (DRT) can only handle one pin per bterm.

This is a known issue with sky130 padframe designs where power pads create multiple bterms for the same power net.

The fix: set `FP_TEMPLATE_COPY_POWER_PINS: false` to not copy the multiple VDDIO pins from the padframe DEF template.

But wait - the config says `FP_TEMPLATE_COPY_POWER_PINS: true`. And we said we shouldn't modify the config.json (nc_chipgen generated it). However, this is a critical flow issue.

Actually, since we're at step 38/DRT now and we need to restart from --from OpenROAD.DetailedRouting, but the ODB already has the multiple VDDIO pins baked in from step 23 (applydeftemplate with copy power pins), we have an issue.

The error `[DRT-0302] Unsupported multiple pins on bterm VDDIO` is because the DEF template copied multiple VDDIO pin locations from the padframe. TritonRoute sees VDDIO as having multiple bterm pins and can't route it.

This is a fundamental issue with the flow configuration. The `FP_TEMPLATE_COPY_POWER_PINS: true` setting is causing TritonRoute to fail.

Fix options:
1. Set `FP_TEMPLATE_COPY_POWER_PINS: false` in config.json and restart from floorplan (25+ min for synthesis + floorplan)
2. Or try to patch the ODB to remove duplicate VDDIO bterm pins

message:
Running command: # Running! SRAM matching visible - floorplan phase.
# Monitor through to DRT and GDS with 2-min polls.
WRAPPER_RUN="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
LOG="/tmp/librelane_final.log"
LR_PID=655801

for i in $(seq 1 480); do
  sleep 120

  LR_ALIVE=$(ps -p $LR_PID -o pid --no-headers 2>/dev/null | xargs)
  ELAPSED=$(ps -p $LR_PID -o etime --no-headers 2>/dev/null | xargs)
  STEP=$(ls -t "$WRAPPER_RUN/" 2>/dev/null | grep "^[0-9]" | head -1)
  STEP_COUNT=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/" 2>/dev/null | grep -c "^[0-9]" || echo 0)
  LOG_BYTES=$(stat -c%s "$LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  LOG_MTIME=$(stat -c%y "$LOG" 2>/dev/null | cut -c12-19)
  LOG_TAIL=$(tail -1 "$LOG" 2>/dev/null | xargs | cut -c1-80)
  FINAL_GDS=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/gds/"*.gds 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  FINAL_LEF=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/lef/"*.lef 2>/dev/null | head -1)

  echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] LR=${LR_ALIVE:-DONE}(${ELAPSED}) step=$STEP (#${STEP_COUNT}) log=$(numfmt --to=iec $LOG_BYTES 2>/dev/null) mtime=$LOG_MTIME"
  echo "  $LOG_TAIL"
  [ -n "$FINAL_GDS" ] && echo "  *** GDS: $FINAL_GDS ***"
  [ -n "$FINAL_LEF" ] && echo "  *** LEF: $FINAL_LEF ***"
  echo ""

  if [ -z "$LR_ALIVE" ]; then
    echo "=== LibreLane EXITED ==="
    ls -lt "$WRAPPER_RUN/" | head -60
    find "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/" 2>/dev/null | sort | head -40
    tail -80 "$LOG"
    break
  fi
done

    tail -80 "$LOG"
    break
  fi
done
